what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG sync icon made from stroked paths showing two opposing curved arrows.
observations: Root svg uses viewBox 0 0 24 24, fill none, and stroke currentColor.
Stroke styling is width 1.5 with round line caps and round joins.
Four path elements draw two arc segments and two angled arrow marks.
standouts: The icon inherits color through currentColor.
The shape reads as a sync or refresh symbol using only path strokes.
conventions: Uses stroke-only icon construction with no filled shapes.
Keeps geometry inline in path d attributes.
notes: A compact reusable SVG asset for a sync action icon.
questions: Is the 1.5 stroke width the intended weight for this icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: The path name suggests a sync icon, and the SVG content draws a sync-style two-arrow mark.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a neutral sync/refresh action glyph that can adapt to surrounding text or UI color.
contribution: Contributes a compact currentColor sync glyph with round-stroked opposing arrows for use in icon-bearing interfaces.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 stroke-only SVG sync icon using four paths, currentColor stroke, no fill, and rounded stroke styling.
intention_for_embedding: It was for representing synchronization or refresh behavior as a reusable design-system icon asset.
